FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Invasive Pests
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: An invasive insect species has been discovered in the forests near your settlements. The pests bore into tree trunks, killing mature timber and disrupting the wildlife that depends on the forest ecosystem. Logging operations are threatened and animal populations are declining. How do you respond?

FactionColonies.FCOptionDef.label: Burn the infested sections. It's drastic, but it will stop the spread.
Import natural predators. Let biology solve the problem.
Do nothing. The forest has survived worse. It will adapt.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Controlled Burn
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: Fire crews set controlled burns through the infested sections of forest. The pests are eradicated quickly, but the scorched areas will take a long time to regrow.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Predator Introduction
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: Imported predator insects are released into the forest. They take to their new environment eagerly, hunting the invasive pests with efficient ruthlessness. The disruption to the ecosystem leaves a lasting impact; while the imported predators successfully get rid of the pests, the predators themselves become a lasting, invasive issue.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Unchecked Infestation
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: Without intervention, the pests spread unchecked through the forest. Mature trees die standing, their hollowed trunks collapsing without warning. Wildlife flees the devastated areas. Logging becomes dangerous and animal husbandry suffers as the ecosystem unravels. The forest will eventually recover, but the process is agonizingly slow.
